0.q: the expression level of the RrHKT1 gene was significantly higher [in silenced plants], suggesting that RrWRKY1 is involved in the regulation of salt tolerance in R. rugosa

1.q: The 62SK-RrWRKY56 co-infiltration with 0800LUC-PROMOTER reduced luminescence obviously compared to empty vector controls. The qPCR analysis confirmed significant downregulation of RrUSP in RrWRKY56-overexpressing plants.

2.q: The expression levels of the RrNHX1, RrABF2, RrRD22, and RrNCED1 genes in the silenced plants were significantly lower than those in the CK and TRV::00 groups

3.q: The expression levels of the RrNHX1, RrABF2, RrRD22, and RrNCED1 genes in the silenced plants were significantly lower than those in the CK and TRV::00 groups

4.q: The expression levels of the RrNHX1, RrABF2, RrRD22, and RrNCED1 genes in the silenced plants were significantly lower than those in the CK and TRV::00 groups

5.q: The expression levels of the RrNHX1, RrABF2, RrRD22, and RrNCED1 genes in the silenced plants were significantly lower than those in the CK and TRV::00 groups

6.q: The interaction assays showed significant growth of USP-PROMOTER-pHIS2 + AD-RrWRKY56 transformants on SD/-His/-Leu/-Trp medium, establishing specific in vivo binding of RrWRKY56 to the RrUSP promoter region.
